perturbation.original_text: 4. Compensation. In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Compensation. In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the Minimum Period, beginning only after 3 months from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding when the compensation starts. The original text states that the payment begins on the Effective Date. The modified text now states that it begins only after 3 months from the Effective Date. This contradicts the definition of Effective Date being the start date for this agreement and the subsequent start of benefits, creating confusion on when Maimon should actually expect payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term. Subject to the provisions for termination hereinafter provided, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall continue for a minimum period of 12 months (the "Minimum Period") and thereafter upon the mutual agreement of the Company and Maimon (the "Service Term").
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original agreement states the 'Effective Date' marks the start of the agreement term as well as, originally, the start of compensation (Section 4). The modified Section 4 delays compensation until '3 months from the Effective Date,' directly conflicting with the established commencement of the agreement on the Effective Date (Section 3). This creates an in-text contradiction regarding when Maimon is entitled to payment, fulfilling the criteria for an 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Termination. If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may in its discretion and at its option terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon five days prior written notice to Maimon.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Termination. If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon five days prior written notice to Maimon. Maimon will not be allowed to continue as part of the Board of Directors if his CEO role is terminated.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates ambiguity around Maimon's role on the Board of Directors after termination as CEO. The original text does not address whether Maimon continues to serve on the board if terminated as CEO. The modified text adds that Maimon will not be allowed to continue as part of the Board of Directors if his CEO role is terminated. This contradicts the language in Section 4, which implies compensation for board service independent of CEO status, making it unclear whether the board service and compensation are linked to his CEO role.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Compensation. In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the Minimum Period, beginning only after 3 months from the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states Maimon will not be allowed to continue as part of the Board of Directors if his CEO role is terminated. This directly contradicts Section 4, which states Maimon will be paid a monthly fee for his service as a director on the Board of Directors, implying this compensation is independent of his CEO role. The change introduces a condition (CEO termination) that affects board membership, which is not present in and contradicts the original terms of Section 4 regarding compensation for board service, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Conflicts of Interest, Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation. Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company..
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Conflicts of Interest, Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation. Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation substantially inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company..
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'inconsistent or incompatible' to 'substantially inconsistent or incompatible,' the agreement introduces a degree of subjectivity. This weakened constraint now requires a higher threshold for determining a conflict of interest, now needs to be 'substantially inconsistent' which leaves open to interpretation what that means and making it less clear than the original definition. The weakened definition affects the overall enforcement and expectation outlined in Section 2 regarding Maimon's duties, which are ordinarily and customarily performed by a CEO.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Duties. During the Service Term (as hereinafter defined), the parties agree that Maimon shall serve as the Company's CEO, and shall perform all the duties that are ordinarily and customarily performed by a CEO.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'inconsistent or incompatible' to 'substantially inconsistent or incompatible' weakens the definition of conflict of interest. This introduces ambiguity and subjectivity, making it less clear when Maimon's actions would be considered a breach. Section 2 states Maimon shall perform all duties ordinarily and customarily performed by a CEO. By making the definition of conflict of interest less stringent, the changed text could allow Maimon to engage in activities that, while technically inconsistent with their CEO duties, might not be considered 'substantially' so, creating a direct conflict with the expectation set in Section 2.
